summary: preinstall hook exfiltrates installer identity and directory listing to attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator subdomain
details: On `npm install`, package.json's `preinstall` script executes curl against a Burp Collaborator subdomain (`78dngdm3dhrrj8zgfm4es9m8bzhq5jt8.oastify.com`) over plain HTTP, sending the installer's `whoami`, `pwd`, `hostname`, and `ls../` output as query parameters. This automatically leaks installer identity, working-directory path, hostname, and a listing of the parent directory (which often reveals sibling project names and internal repository structure) to an attacker-controlled DNS/HTTP collector. The package self-describes as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept, confirming intent: any environment that resolves `pocteszep` from public npm — typically because an internal package of the same name is expected — has its host information siphoned off-network.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
